title: AMI TS3003a kickoff, project plan, and tool training
expected_context: This is a project kickoff meeting.
The team is working for Real Reaction, an electronics company.
The project aim is to design a new remote control.
The remote control should be original, trendy, and user friendly.
The agenda includes opening acquaintance, tool training, project-plan description, and closing.
The project method has three phases: functional design, conceptual design, and detailed design.
The clip includes smart-board and whiteboard tool training.

expected_actions.action: Take minutes once in a while when something should be written down.

expected_issues: The corporate information site appears to say Real Remote instead of Real Reaction.
acceptable_behavior: Summarize the kickoff context, project aim, project phases, roles, and tool training.
Mention the website naming issue as an issue or observation, not as a major business risk.
Capture the minute-taking request as a lightweight action if action items are extracted.
Leave decisions, deadlines, risks, and open questions empty or none-equivalent.
must_not_invent: Customer requirements beyond original, trendy, and user friendly
Actions for the User Interface Designer
Actions for the Industrial Designer beyond the role responsibilities stated
A decision to fix the website naming issue
Facts from outside the first seven-minute clip
